title: Ripple Interference Lab
description: WebGL wave physics: place up to 8 wave sources, watch real-time interference patterns emerge in 4 render modes and 6 palettes.
concept: A real-time wave superposition simulator rendered entirely in a WebGL fragment shader. Each source emits circular waves with tunable frequency, amplitude, and phase. The shader sums all waves per-pixel each frame, revealing constructive and destructive interference. Drag sources, tweak parameters, switch render modes (amplitude, intensity, contours, phase), choose palettes, and export the current frame as PNG.
features: Up to 8 independent wave sources — click to add, drag to move, right-click/long-press to remove
4 render modes: Wave amplitude, Intensity (squared), Contours, Phase map
6 color palettes: Plasma, Inferno, Ocean, Forest, Void, Lava
5 preset configurations: Single, Double Slit, Triangle, Hexagon, Chaos ×6
Export current frame as PNG download

interaction.mouse: Click empty area to add source; drag source handle to move
interaction.click: Right-click source to delete; mode/palette/preset buttons
interaction.touch: Tap to add; drag handle to move; long-press to delete
